subject: First Aid & Safety
title: White Blood Cells in Action
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: White Blood Cells in Action.

hook.text: White blood cells eat bacteria alive. Your immune system runs on controlled violence.

buildup.text: Neutrophils are the first white blood cells to arrive at a wound—within minutes.

discovery.text: They engulf and digest bacteria through phagocytosis—literally 'cell eating.'

twist.text: Pus is the aftermath: dead neutrophils, destroyed bacteria, and tissue debris.

climax.text: Macrophages arrive next, cleaning up debris and signaling tissue repair to begin.

punchline.text: Pus looks gross but it means your immune system won the battle.

quiz.question: What is pus made of?
options: Dead white blood cells, bacteria, and debris
